+---
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9180'
+title: 'Rich session event stream: text/reasoning/toolCall/toolStatus/compaction/turnEnded'
+---
+Harness-collapse item (harness plan §4 HarnessEvent absorbed). Add an event-element variant of streamResponse (String-only today): textDelta, reasoningDelta, toolCall(id:name:argumentsJSON:), toolStatus(id:status:summary:), compaction(CompactionResult), turnEnded(TokenUsage). Correlation ids are load-bearing: two concurrent same-name tool calls distinguishable; ids surface 1:1 to consumers (FoundationModelsACPAgent maps them onto ACP toolCallId). The chokepoint already sees all of this as transcript entries.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,4 +1,25 @@
 ---
+comments:
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01ky9zzj46xp7wmxsvvd9y7ymj
+  text: |-
+    Implemented. Research: RoutedSession.streamResponse(to:maxTokens:) is String-only, implemented directly on RoutedSessionActor (not a protocol-extension default). The chokepoint (generate -> runTurn -> finishTurnAndRequeueIfUnattached -> finishTurn -> recordTranscriptDelta) already computes a per-turn TranscriptDiffer diff before persisting; that's the single source of truth this task taps.
+
+    Added:
+    - Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Session/SessionEvent.swift: new public `SessionEvent` enum (textDelta, reasoningDelta, toolCall(id:name:argumentsJSON:), toolStatus(id:status:summary:), compaction(CompactionResult), turnEnded(TokenUsage)), plus `ToolCallStatus` (running/completed/failed) and `TokenUsage` (tokensIn/tokensOut).
+    - RoutedSession protocol: new `streamEvents(to:maxTokens:) -> AsyncThrowingStream<SessionEvent, Error>` requirement + `streamEvents(to:)` convenience default-maxTokens overload, mirroring streamResponse's existing shape exactly.
+    - RoutedSessionActor: `streamEvents`/`streamEventsGenerating` (text chunks -> .textDelta live, same as streamResponse), plus a new `onEvent: ((SessionEvent) -> Void)?` parameter threaded through generate/runTurn/finishTurnAndRequeueIfUnattached/finishTurn/recordTranscriptDelta (default nil everywhere, so respond/streamResponse/dispatchNextPrompt are byte-identical to before). `recordTranscriptDelta`'s diff loop now also calls a new static `emitSessionEvents` helper per diff partial: `.toolCalls` -> toolCall + toolStatus(.running) per call; `.toolOutput` -> toolStatus(.completed, summary: flattened text), correlated by id (relies on Apple's SDK invariant that ToolOutput.id == the ToolCall.id it answers, same invariant TranscriptEntryMapper already assumes); `.reasoning` -> reasoningDelta. Any toolCall id with no matching toolOutput by the end of the diff -> toolStatus(.failed). `finishTurn` emits turnEnded(TokenUsage) once usage is known (mirrors the existing tokensIn/tokensOut-available gate), so it fires on both the success and throwing exits, in-order after all diff-derived events for that turn.
+    - Extracted a private static `wrapAsyncStream<Element>(_:)` helper (needed `@Sendable` on the closure param for Swift 6 strict concurrency) so streamResponse/streamEvents share the AsyncThrowingStream/Task/onTermination scaffolding instead of duplicating it — this came out of the local review engine's first pass (see below).
+    - Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/SessionEventStreamTests.swift: 9 new tests using a ScriptedTranscriptBackend (mirrors TranscriptFidelityTests.VariableTranscriptBackend — entries set directly by the test, no auto-append) covering: plain textDelta-only turn, tool call requested+answered (running->completed with output as summary), two concurrent same-name tool calls distinguished by id, a dangling tool call reported failed, reasoning entries, turnEnded present/absent based on backend usage reporting, a throwing turn that still yields events recorded before the throw, and that streamEvents persists the exact same transcript shape streamResponse does.
+
+    Deliberate scope decision: `.compaction(CompactionResult)` is in the enum (matches the task's literal spec) but nothing currently emits it — compact() is a separate caller-driven call not wired into streamEvents. Confirmed via sibling backlog tasks (8213x39 "auto-compaction opt-in", ekd82f4 "@Observable per-session state" which explicitly wants a "compacting" phase) that a future auto-fold task is expected to call onEvent?(.compaction(result)) through this same mechanism once it lands — this task only had to build the plumbing + wire the currently-available cases. Documented this explicitly in SessionEvent.swift's doc comment so it doesn't read as dead code.
+
+    Local review engine (mcp__sah__review) flagged two things: (1) a pre-existing `replace(_:prompt:)` argument-label finding unrelated to this diff — confirmed via `git log --grep replace` that this was already investigated and deliberately left alone in commit d749daf, not touched here; (2) streamResponse/streamEvents duplicating AsyncThrowingStream/Task scaffolding — fixed by extracting `wrapAsyncStream`. Re-ran review after the fix: only a minor test-file nesting-depth nit remains in ScriptedTranscriptBackend.streamResponse, which is left as-is because it's a verbatim mirror of the established StubSessionBackend.streamResponse/VariableTranscriptBackend.streamResponse idiom already used across this test suite (capturing immutable local copies of mutable class properties before returning the AsyncThrowingStream closure) — matching it keeps this new test file consistent with its siblings rather than introducing a one-off style.
+
+    TDD note: given the multi-file threading (5 function signatures touched in one chokepoint), tests were written alongside the implementation rather than strictly red-then-green per function; I did do one manual regression check (temporarily short-circuited emitSessionEvents to a no-op) to confirm the tests would in fact fail without the derivation logic, then reverted — full formal per-test red/green wasn't practical given the shared chokepoint threading, but the tests are real (non-tautological) and independently verified via that spot-check.
+
+    Verified via clean `swift build`/`swift build --build-tests` (zero warnings) and `swift test` (521 tests: 498 main + 18 integration [gated, skipped] + 5 evals, all passing — baseline was 512, +9 new). Adversarial double-check agent dispatched per really-done; will report its verdict separately.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-24T12:00:41.606483+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KY7E2TP9DBGFV8RJJJKDAE4B
 position_column: doing

@@ -1,25 +1,4 @@

Documented this explicitly in SessionEvent.swift's doc comment so it doesn't read as dead code.

Re-ran review after the fix: only a minor test-file nesting-depth nit remains in ScriptedTranscriptBackend.streamResponse, which is left as-is because it's a verbatim mirror of the established StubSessionBackend.streamResponse/VariableTranscriptBackend.streamResponse idiom already used across this test suite (capturing immutable local copies of mutable class properties before returning the AsyncThrowingStream closure) — matching it keeps this new test file consistent with its siblings rather than introducing a one-off style.
-
-    TDD note: given the multi-file threading (5 function signatures touched in one chokepoint), tests were written alongside the implementation rather than strictly red-then-green per function; I did do one manual regression check (temporarily short-circuited emitSessionEvents to a no-op) to confirm the tests would in fact fail without the derivation logic, then reverted — full formal per-test red/green wasn't practical given the shared chokepoint threading, but the tests are real (non-tautological) and independently verified via that spot-check.
-
-    Verified via clean `swift build`/`swift build --build-tests` (zero warnings) and `swift test` (521 tests: 498 main + 18 integration [gated, skipped] + 5 evals, all passing — baseline was 512, +9 new). Adversarial double-check agent dispatched per really-done; will report its verdict separately.
